PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO COPIES OF THE DVD'S AND CD'S AS THE PRIOR ORDERS OF THE COURT ONLY PROVIDE FOR EITHER PRODUCTION BY DEFENDANT OR INSPECTION BY PLAINTIFF OF SPECIFIED COMPLAINTS. WHILE THE PRIOR ORDERS DO NOT IMPOSE ANY GEOGRAPHICAL LIMITS ON THE COMPLAINTS THAT MAY BE INSPECTED BY PLAINTIFF, PURSUANT TO THE PREVIOUS AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, THE ACTUAL PRODUCTION OF COMPLAINTS IS LIMITED TO THOSE COMPLAINTS MADE BY PERSONS IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO REDACT PERSONAL IDENTIFYING INFORMATION FROM THE COMPLAINTS OF PERSONS IN CALIFORNIA DESIGNATED BY PLAINTIFF FOR COPYING AS THE PREVIOUS ORDERS OF THE COURT PROVIDE FOR NO SUCH LIMITATION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO WHETHER ALTERNATIVE MEANS OF COPYING DESIGNATED COMPLAINTS WOULD REDUCE THE COST AND THE INCONVENIENCE TO THE PARTIES. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
